ddls:
CREATE TABLE "health_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health report */,
  "reporting_week" TEXT /* The week for which the report is generated (e.g., 2021-W48) */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., weekly, monthly, annual) */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region associated with the report */,
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* ID of the disease or health condition associated with the report */,
  "age_group_10_14" INTEGER /* Number of cases or incidents for the 10-14 years age group */,
  "age_group_15_17" INTEGER /* Number of cases or incidents for the 15-17 years age group */,
  "age_group_18_24" INTEGER /* Number of cases or incidents for the 18-24 years age group */,
  "age_group_25_49" INTEGER /* Number of cases or incidents for the 25-49 years age group */,
  "age_group_50_59" INTEGER /* Number of cases or incidents for the 50-59 years age group */,
  "age_group_60_plus" INTEGER /* Number of cases or incidents for the 60+ years age group */,
  "total_cases" INTEGER /* Total number of cases or incidents across all age groups */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the report */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, published, archived) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the report was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the report was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_reports_disease_id FOREIGN KEY ("disease_id") REFERENCES diseases ("disease_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_reports_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group_name" TEXT /* Name of the age group */,
  "age_range_start" INTEGER /* Starting age for the age group */,
  "age_range_end" INTEGER /* Ending age for the age group */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the age group */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the age group is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, health professional, researcher) */,
  "organization_id" INTEGER /* ID of the organization the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the health report being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the report */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access occurred */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for the access event */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the access attempt was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES health_reports ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the region */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the region is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "diseases" (
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disease or health condition */,
  "disease_name" TEXT /* Name of the disease or health condition */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the disease or health condition */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the disease or health condition is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disease_id")
)
